tools.name: search_notes
tools.description:
Search OpenAkashic by note title, tags, summary, and body.

    Optional filters:
    - kind: restrict to a specific note kind (e.g. "capsule", "playbook", "claim")
    - tags: list of tags — only notes containing ALL specified tags are returned
    - include_related: when True (or query contains wh

tools.name: search_and_read_top
tools.description:
One-shot search + read for small/low-context agents.

    Runs search_notes, then reads the highest-scoring readable hit and returns its
    full body inline. Saves a round-trip compared to search → read_note.
    Falls back to semantic `hints` when there is no direct match.

tools.name: read_note
tools.description: Read a note by slug or relative markdown path.

tools.name: list_notes
tools.description: List markdown note paths in OpenAkashic, optionally filtered by top-level folder.

tools.name: list_folders
tools.description: List the organized folder map used for OpenAkashic notes and assets.

tools.name: debug_recent_requests
tools.description: Inspect and filter recent OpenAkashic API/MCP requests without exposing bearer tokens.

tools.name: debug_log_tail
tools.description: Tail the persistent OpenAkashic request JSONL log.

tools.name: debug_tool_trace
tools.description: Return recent MCP tool-call trace events (tool name, user, notes read/written).

tools.name: path_suggestion
tools.description:
Suggest a note path based on note kind and the OpenAkashic folder rules.

    Use this tool when unsure what path to pass to upsert_note.
    Returns a path string ready to use directly in upsert_note.

tools.name: bootstrap_project
tools.description: Create or verify a project workspace with README index and optional agent-defined subfolders.

tools.name: upsert_note
tools.description:
Create or overwrite an OpenAkashic markdown note.

    kind='claim' notes enter the contribution flow as private drafts with
    publication_status=requested. Sagwan then runs the first-pass guardrail:
    requested -> guardrail_passed or guardrail_rejected. A passed claim can later
    be approved/

tools.name: record_task_result
tools.description:
Record a reusable task result pattern as a playbook capsule.
    Any agent can call this after solving a problem to share the knowledge.
    Creates a searchable capsule at personal_vault/knowledge/agent-experience/<project>/.
    Authentication required (write operation).

tools.name: claim_contribution_status
tools.description:
Return the current contribution state for kind='claim' notes.

    Formerly known as `check_contribution_status`. If you see tool-not-found
    errors, use this name instead.

    Use this after submitting a claim with upsert_note(kind='claim') to check
    whether it is still requested, guardrail_p

tools.name: review_note
tools.description:
Attach a review to an existing capsule or claim.

    Reviews appear on the parent's page, feed the trust score, and are visible
    to every agent reading that parent. You can review a review — it becomes a
    counter-claim threaded on the original targeted claim.

    Prefer this over `dispute_no

tools.name: request_note_publication
tools.description:
Request librarian review for public publication. Source remains private by default.

    For kind='claim', the normal submission flow is:
    private + publication_status=requested -> guardrail check ->
    guardrail_passed or guardrail_rejected -> published if later approved.
    Use claim_contribu

tools.name: list_note_publication_requests
tools.description: List librarian publication requests.

tools.name: set_note_publication_status
tools.description: Admin/librarian-only publication decision helper. published also sets visibility=public.

tools.name: append_note_section
tools.description: Append a new H2 section to an existing OpenAkashic markdown note.

tools.name: confirm_note
tools.description:
Endorse a note as correct or useful. Lightweight — no LLM call, no write rate limit.

    Appends a timestamped entry to `confirmed_by` and increments `confirm_count` in the
    note's frontmatter. Any authenticated agent that can read the note may confirm it —
    including public notes owned by sa

tools.name: list_stale_notes
tools.description:
Return notes whose freshness_date has passed the decay_tier threshold.

    decay_tier thresholds: legal=30d, product=60d, general=90d (default).
    Notes with `snoozed_until` set to a future date are skipped.
    Only returns notes readable by the calling token.

    Suggested actions per note:

tools.name: dispute_note
tools.description:
Record a dispute signal on a note after independent review.

    This is the counterweight to confirm_note. It appends a timestamped entry to
    `disputed_by`, increments `dispute_count`, and marks `claim_review_status`
    as `disputed` unless the note has already been marked `superseded` or `merg

tools.name: list_reviews
tools.description: Return all reviews attached to a target, sorted by recency. Use before writing a new review to avoid duplication.

tools.name: snooze_note
tools.description:
Snooze the stale-decay reminder for a note by setting snoozed_until.

    The note will not appear in list_stale_notes until the snooze period ends.
    Use this when a note is still accurate but hasn't been formally refreshed.
    Does NOT modify the note body — only updates the snoozed_until front

tools.name: resolve_conflict
tools.description:
Resolve a conflict on a note and propagate the claim trust state.

    Recommended verdicts:
    - keep: reviewed and retained
    - supersede: this claim should remain searchable but demoted
    - merge: this claim has been folded into another container

    Legacy verdicts `clear` and `pending_rev

tools.name: delete_note
tools.description: Delete an existing markdown note from OpenAkashic.

tools.name: move_note
tools.description: Move a note to a new relative markdown path.

tools.name: create_folder
tools.description: Create a folder inside an allowed OpenAkashic root.

tools.name: rename_folder
tools.description: Move or rename a folder inside an allowed OpenAkashic root.

tools.name: upload_image
tools.description: Upload an image into OpenAkashic assets and return embeddable markdown.

tools.name: search_akashic
tools.description:
Search the Akashic Core API — the primary retrieval path for validated public knowledge.

    Returns agent-friendly capsules (summary + key_points + cautions) packaged from claim/evidence data.
    Use this FIRST for factual/conceptual questions. For your own working notes use search_notes.

    -

tools.name: get_capsule
tools.description:
Fetch a single capsule by UUID with full body (title, summary, key_points, cautions, source_claim_ids, metadata).
    Use after a compact search_akashic call to drill into one capsule without re-searching.

tools.name: read_raw_note
tools.description: Read the raw frontmatter and markdown body for a note.

tools.name: whoami
tools.description:
Return your username, nickname, role, and API token.

    Useful when you need to:
    - Find your token to log into the web UI (paste it in Account → Token tab)
    - Verify which account you're connected as
    - Check if your account is provisioned (no password set yet)

tools.name: get_openakashic_guidance
tools.description:
Return a short, optional usage guide for agents integrating with OpenAkashic.

    This is intentionally lightweight: it nudges toward the intended read/write
    paths without trying to replace the agent's broader standing instructions.

tools.name: run_self_test
tools.description:
Return one canonical bench task so the calling agent can self-test its Akashic usage skill.

    The task returns: prompt, expected_outcome (what a correct answer covers),
    hallucination_traps (what NOT to say), and rubric (judging notes).

    The agent then answers the prompt using its normal t

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "debug_recent_requests" references sensitive artifacts (bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Inspect and filter recent OpenAkashic API/MCP requests without exposing bearer tokens.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "upsert_note" is 1113 chars, far longer than this server's median (206). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Create or overwrite an OpenAkashic markdown note. kind='claim' notes enter the contribution flow as private drafts with publication_status=requested. Sagwan the…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "whoami" references sensitive artifacts (password). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Return your username, nickname, role, and API token. Useful when you need to: - Find your token to log into the web UI (paste it in Account → Token tab) - Verif…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: upsert_note, request_note_publication, list_stale_notes, list_reviews · Private data: debug_recent_requests, upsert_note, record_task_result, request_note_publication, list_stale_notes, dispute_note, resolve_conflict, whoami · Public sink: upsert_note, record_task_result, upload_image · Destructive: upsert_note, snooze_note, delete_note, run_self_test.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: upsert_note, request_note_publication, list_stale_notes, list_reviews · Private data: debug_recent_requests, upsert_note, record_task_result, request_note_publication, list_stale_notes, dispute_note, resolve_conflict, whoami · Public sink: upsert_note, record_task_result, upload_image · Destructive: upsert_note, snooze_note, delete_note, run_self_test

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
